signals.headline: **Headline PCE inflation** rose to 3
signals.raw: **Headline PCE inflation** rose to 3.45% year-over-year[1], exceeding earlier expectations and prompting Goldman Sachs to raise its December 2026 headline PCE forecast by a full percentage point since the war began[1].

signals.headline: **Short-term inflation expectations shifted dramatically**: The University of Michigan survey jumped one percentage poin
signals.raw: **Short-term inflation expectations shifted dramatically**: The University of Michigan survey jumped one percentage point to 4.8% for the year ahead, while financial market-implied expectations for 2026 headline CPI inflation rose 0.8 percentage points to 3.3%[1].

signals.headline: Broader implications:** Goldman forecasts oil to pull back to $80 per barrel by Q4 2026, but explicitly notes upside ris
signals.raw: Broader implications:** Goldman forecasts oil to pull back to $80 per barrel by Q4 2026, but explicitly notes upside risks remain[1]. The conflict has also raised Gulf export prices including fertilizer and aluminum, with Goldman estimating higher fertilizer costs will add roughly 0.1 percentage point to headline inflation this year[1]. signals.headline: **Core inflation showed mixed signals**: Core CPI came in at 2
signals.raw: **Core inflation showed mixed signals**: Core CPI came in at 2.6% year-over-year with a modest 0.2% monthly gain—better than the 0.3% expected—suggesting underlying price pressures remain contained despite energy turbulence[2].
